title: V-1 thermal detonator

The '''V-1 thermal detonator''' was a small [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonator]] manufactured by [[Merr-Sonn Munitions, Inc./Legends|Merr-Sonn Munitions, Inc.]] for service on the battlefield, becoming the primary grenade of choice for both sides of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].

==Description==
Specifically designed for battlefield deployment, the V-1 thermal detonator was of smaller caliber than more typical thermal detonators. Besides being more portable for soldiers, this effectively limited the size of the grenade's explosions, reducing the risk of friendly casualties. In spite of this, as a military model, its power was calibrated to maximize enemy casualties within its blast radius.<ref name="BF1 guide" /><ref name="BF2 guide" />

It bore a similar design to the [[Class-A thermal detonator/Legends|Class-A thermal detonator]] also manufactured by Merr-Sonn Munitions but had a slate-[[Color/Legends|gray]] shell instead of the Class-A's reflective shell.<ref name="LucasArts">{{LucasArts|url=games/swbattlefront/index.html|text=''Star Wars: Battlefront''}}</ref>

==History==
During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the V-1 thermal detonator was officially contracted to the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]], whose [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army]] employed them, alongside the [[V-6 haywire grenade]], as the standard grenades of its [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]]. Unlike the V-6, clones of all units were issued the V-1, including [[Clone sharpshooter/Legends|clone sharpshooters]], [[Clone jet trooper/Legends|clone jet troopers]], [[Clone heavy assault trooper|clone heavy troopers]], and [[Advanced Recon Commando/Legends|Advanced Recon Commandos]].<ref name="BF1 guide" /><ref name="BF2 guide" />

While the Republic was its official buyer, V-1 thermal detonators were widely sighted on the battlefield in the hands of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. Specialized [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-Series battle droids]] such as the [[assault battle droid]] and the [[battle droid assassin]] utilized them as part of their primary equipment, just like their clone counterparts. Responding to these reports, Merr-Sonn Munitions was keen on denying any accusations as to the sale of the thermal detonators to the enemy of the Republic.<ref name="BF1 guide" /><ref name="BF2 guide" />
